behavior_summary: Provides the NPC/object 'U8P_TROLL::look' (examine) handler for the Troll entity. When the player inspects or 'looks at' the Troll, the handler emits a short bark (spoken text) containing the token "troll " and then suspends/ends the U8P_TROLL::look process.
There is no branching, no menu, no state reads or writes, and no external function calls; the behavior is purely a short vocal response to an examine event.
Unnamed raw/decompiler nodes appear around the bark and suspend steps but do not change the observable gameplay effect: a one-line bark on U8P_TROLL::look.
notable_control_flow: U8P_TROLL::look executes as a process and immediately issues a single Bark line ('troll ') and then suspends; there are no conditional branches or loops.
Execution is short-lived: bark -> suspend (end of U8P_TROLL::look process).
open_questions: Is the bark text intentionally the single token 'troll ' including a trailing space, or is that a formatting artifact from extraction?

Does the process receive an implicit actor/context parameter (the examining player or NPC) from the engine even though no explicit parameters are declared? (This is typical for U8P_TROLL::look handlers but not visible in the extracted nodes.)

main_function.name: U8P_TROLL::look
main_function.purpose: Handle the player's 'U8P_TROLL::look' or examine action on the Troll by producing a short bark/description and ending the U8P_TROLL::look process.
entry_conditions: Triggered when the player/avatar examines or 'looks at' the Troll NPC/object (inferred from function classification 'U8P_TROLL::look' and process type 0x0208).
Invoked by the engine's U8P_TROLL::look/examine event for this entity.

function_map.name: U8P_TROLL::look
function_map.role: Respond to examine/U8P_TROLL::look events by emitting a single bark ('troll ') and suspending the process.
